Empires & Puzzles Costumed Quenell Review: The Wolf Champion Returns
Quenell is back with a fresh coat of paint and a massive stat injection. Is the new 'Champion of the Wolves' just a cosmetic upgrade, or does she finally have the bite to match her bark?
Costumed Quenell: New Fur, Sharper Teeth

The Clash of Knights event has returned, bringing a much-needed costume for Quenell. Once a feared sniper, she had fallen behind due to severe power creep. This new "Champion of the Wolves" costume doesn't just give her a makeover; it injects her with modern stats and a crucial survivability tool: self-healing. This review focuses on the
, analyzing whether these upgrades are enough to bring her back to the top of the food chain.

The costume transforms Quenell from a wild ranger into a regal champion. She trades her leather scraps for polished armor and a majestic wolf pelt pauldron. It’s a "glow-up" in the truest sense—she looks less like she sleeps in a ditch and more like she commands the pack. The visual fidelity is sharper, matching the modern art style of recent releases.
The difference is night and day.
A massive jump that puts her in the same weight class as recent heroes.
With the costume bonus and Limit Breaks, she can easily surpass 1400 Attack, making her hits devastating.
The boost to Defense (1121) and Health (2114) means she can actually take a hit from a modern sniper without evaporating. This is the single most important upgrade.
"Rusty Blade" is a refined version of her original skill, trading raw potential for consistency.
to three targets immediately (up from 320%). This higher floor is excellent for fast kills.

She gains +30% power per turn (capped at +150%), compared to the base's +40% (capped at +200%). While the theoretical max is lower (510% vs 520%), the higher base attack stat of the costume means she will likely deal
actual damage in almost every scenario.
is a game-changer. It allows her to recover from slash attacks and DoTs, keeping her alive long enough to fire a second volley.
This "base" hit is already higher than the
hit of the non-limit broken original Quenell.
(360% + 150%) = 510% x 1457 = ~7430 damage value. This is nuclear.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The updated Wolf Family bonus now applies to 1+ heroes, giving a 20%-50% chance to make buffs undispellable. This is fantastic for solo usage, ensuring her Attack Ramp and Heal over Time aren't easily wiped.
75% chance for +1 turn, 25% for +2 turns. This is amazing for her mechanics.
, giving you a larger window to fire the second shot before the buff expires.
"Special Boost" increases her special skill damage. Combined with her high base stats and ramping multiplier, this adds another layer of lethality.
This costume is a textbook example of how to revitalize an old hero. It fixes her main weakness (stats/fragility) while preserving her identity (ramping damage).

She is a strong, fast hit-3 hero. While she lacks the utility of some "God-tier" heroes (like those who silence or steal mana), her sheer damage output demands respect. She is a top pick for War Attack teams and extremely dangerous in Rush Wars.
Ranger Class (Bypass) is the only advantage.
Better stats, higher initial damage, self-healing, better passive, Monk class (withstand ailments).

. The stats and self-heal outweigh the Ranger bypass in almost every scenario. If you desperately need a bypass Ranger for a specific counter (e.g., against a riposte team), you can toggle back, but generally, the Costume is the daily driver.
Focus on Defense and Health nodes.
She already hits like a truck. This path ensures she lives to use her self-heal.
Maximizes the 510% multiplier for highlight-reel damage.
Still susceptible to being one-shot by top-tier snipers.
. She is a damage dealer first and foremost. Her self-heal provides the sustain; your emblems should ensure the kill.
She can finally take a hit.
600 HP over 3 turns (potentially extended by passive) is significant sustain.
360% fast hit to three is a strong baseline, even without the ramp.
Despite the Family Bonus chance, her buffs can still be dispelled, neutralizing her ramp and heal.
She heals only herself. She doesn't support the team beyond killing enemies.
Self-sustain makes her great for long battles or multiple waves.
. The double healing (her own + healer) makes her very hard to pin down.
. Defense down into Quenell's hit wipes the board.
Heroes like Hippo or silent heroes prevent her from gaining her Ramp buff.
If you pull this costume, prioritize it. A fast, self-sustaining sniper will carry you through almost all map stages and low-tier raids.

Costume Quenell is a worthy investment of tonics. She fits perfectly into modern "Hit-3" metas. While she doesn't have the crazy utility of the latest Goblins or Constructs, pure damage at this speed is never bad.
"Finally, she doesn't die when I look at her wrong."
"The self-heal is the real MVP of this costume. It makes her an actual threat instead of a glass cannon."
"I miss the Ranger class, but the stats are too good to ignore."

Costumed Quenell is a triumphant return for the Wolf Champion. She hits harder, lives longer, and looks better doing it. She proves that you don't always need complex new mechanics to be good—sometimes, you just need to be really, really good at punching people in the face.
A solid, A-tier damage dealer who has successfully clawed her way back into relevance.
A detailed review of Costumed Quenell, the revitalized 5-star Nature hero. We analyze her massive stat boost, self-healing capabilities, and how she compares to her original form in the modern meta.
